summary: One unit test failed during the hibernate-core test task: DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion failed an assertion about owner version incrementing.
root_cause: The test org.hibernate.orm.test.version.db.DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion failed with an assertion: it expected the owner version not to be incremented but the owner version was incremented (assertFalse expected false but was true).

evidence.reason: Shows which test failed and that it failed during the Gradle test run.

evidence.quote: org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>
evidence.reason: The exact assertion failure message indicates the test expected false (no increment) but observed true (version incremented).
Stack trace points to the failing test method and the source line number where the assertion failed.
Confirms there was a single failing test in the whole test run.
Build failure was caused by failing tests in the hibernate-core test task.

evidence.quote: There were failing tests. See the report at: file:///home/runner/work/hibernate-orm/hibernate-orm/hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html
evidence.reason: Indicates where the full test report is available for investigation.

suggested_fix: Open the test report at 'hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html' and inspect org.hibernate.orm.test.version.db.DbVersionTest (line 60) to reproduce the failure. Run only that test locally (or via Gradle) to debug why the owner version is being incremented (verify the setup and the code path that updates versioning). Based on findings, either fix the production code that unexpectedly increments the owner version, or adjust the test expectations if the behavior has legitimately changed.
